description_long: This kiosk is a small, single-story structure with a weathered, corrugated metal roof that is rusted and uneven, featuring both solid and striped panels. The walls are painted a faded blue, showing signs of wear and exposure, and rest on a simple rectangular base. The front of the kiosk is open, revealing a shaded interior with two concrete benches and a step leading up from the ground. Wooden posts support the roof overhang at the front, while the sides each have a single, rectangular, wooden-framed window. The overall construction appears utilitarian, with materials and finishes suggesting a focus on function over aesthetics. The kiosk is typical of informal urban or roadside commercial stalls found in many developing regions.
description: A small, blue-painted kiosk with a rusted corrugated metal roof, open front, and simple wooden-framed windows on each side. The structure is utilitarian and weathered, with concrete benches inside and wooden posts supporting the roof.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the rusted, uneven metal roof and the plain blue back wall with no openings or features.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a single rectangular window and the sloped roof, with the wooden post and part of the open front visible.
description_view_2: The front view reveals the open entrance, two concrete benches inside, a step, and the supporting wooden posts under the overhanging roof.
description_view_3: The right side view shows another single rectangular window and the sloped roof, mirroring the left side.
attribute_window_presence: Two rectangular windows, one on each side wall.
attribute_door_configuration: Single open entryway at the front.
attribute_roof_design: Gabled, sloped roof made of rusted corrugated metal sheets with visible overhangs and irregular edges.
